title: Neural network involvement for religious experiences in worship measured by EEG microstate analysis.

assets.abstract: Not much is known about large-scale brain activation patterns in religious states of mind and previous studies have not set an emphasis on experience. The present study investigated the phenomenon of religious experiences through microstate analysis, and it was the first neurocognitive research to tackle the dimension of experience directly. Hence, a total of 60 evangelical Christians participated in an experiment where they were asked to engage in worship and try to connect with God. With a bar

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Bridge paper score=10.0: links Liberation/Enlightenment concept to mechanistic neuroscience
